popularStory.headline: John III Comyn, Lord of Badenoch
popularStory.description: John III Comyn, Lord of Badenoch and Lord of Lochaber, also known simply as the Red Comyn (died 10 February 1306) was a Scottish nobleman who was an important figure in the Wars of Scottish Independen …

zh.Inverlochy Castle, Inverness-shire, Scot: 蘇格蘭音巴內-爾音巴洛 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Comyn Lord of Badenoch於1274年出生在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾巴登時，他的父親John Comyn II32歲，母親Alianora de Balliol28歲。他於1298年與Joan de Valence在英國可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1306年2月10日在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾享年32歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.Inverlochy Castle, Inverness-shire, Scot: 苏格兰音巴內-爾音巴洛 卡素
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Comyn Lord of Badenoch于1274年出生在苏格兰音巴內-爾巴登时，他的父亲John Comyn II32岁，母亲Alianora de Balliol28岁。他于1298年与Joan de Valence在英国可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1306年2月10日在英国苏格兰度福利爾享年32岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Comyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Comyn Lord of Badenoch was born in 1274, in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, his father, John Comyn II, was 32 and his mother, Alianora de Balliol, was 28. He married Joan de Valence in 1298, in Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 10 February 1306, in Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 32, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Comyn Lord of Badenoch est né en 1274, à Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Écosse, son père, John Comyn II, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Alianora de Balliol, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Valence en 1298, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 10 février 1306, à Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Comyn Lord of Badenoch 1274 in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Comyn II 32 und seine Mutter Alianora de Balliol 28. Er hat 1298 in Cornwall, England Joan de Valence geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 10. Februar 1306 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Dumfriesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Comyn Lord of Badenoch è nato il nel 1274 nel Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, il suo padre, John Comyn II, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Alianora de Balliol, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Valence nel 1298, nel Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 10 febbraio 1306, nel Dumfriesshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: John Comyn Lord of Badenochが1274年にスコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアバデノチで生まれた時，父John Comyn IIは32歳，母Alianora de Balliolは28歳であった。1298年にイギリスコルンワルでJoan de Valenceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1306年2月10日にイギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアで32歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Comyn Lord of Badenoch(이)가 1274년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 바데노치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Comyn II(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Alianora de Balliol(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1298년 영국 코른월에서 Joan de Valence(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1306년 2월 10일 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

mn.Inverlochy Castle, Inverness-shire, Scot: Inverlochy Castle, Siorrachd Inbhir Nis, Шотланд

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Comyn Lord of Badenoch nasceu em 1274, em Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, seu pai, John Comyn II, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Alianora de Balliol, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Valence em 1298, em Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de fevereiro de 1306, em Dumfriesshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 32 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Comyn Lord of Badenoch родился в 1274 г. в местности баденох, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John Comyn II было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Alianora de Balliol, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Valence в 1298 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 10 февраля 1306 г. в местности думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Comyn Lord of Badenoch nació en 1274, en Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, su papá, John Comyn II, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Alianora de Balliol, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Joan de Valence en 1298, en Cornwall, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 10 de febrero de 1306, en Dumfriesshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
